rows.v: Basil, Cilantro, Parsley, Mint, Dill, Chives, Tarragon
Added at the end of cooking — heat destroys their delicate aroma quickly.
Tender herbs are added at the very end — their volatile oils flash off with heat, so a basil leaf cooked too long loses everything that makes it basil.
Rosemary, Thyme, Oregano, Sage, Bay Leaf, Marjoram
Stand up to long cooking — add early so their oils infuse the whole dish.
Woody herbs are built for the long haul — rosemary and thyme can simmer for hours, releasing flavor slowly, which is why they anchor stocks, braises, and roasts.
Cinnamon, Nutmeg, Clove, Allspice, Star Anise, Cardamom
The backbone of baking and mulled drinks — also excellent in savory braises.
Nutmeg and mace come from the very same fruit — mace is the lacy red web wrapped around the nutmeg seed, the only spice that yields two distinct seasonings.
Cumin, Coriander Seed, Fennel Seed, Caraway, Mustard Seed, Annatto (Achiote)
Toast briefly in a dry pan before using to wake up their essential oils.
Toasting whole seeds before grinding wakes up their oils — a dry-skillet minute deepens cumin or coriander far more than buying it pre-ground ever could.
Black Pepper, Cayenne, Paprika, Ginger, Sichuan Pepper
Add heat and depth — a little goes a long way, and quality varies enormously by freshness.
Green, black, and white peppercorns all come from one vine — picked at different stages and processed differently, they're the same berry wearing three disguises.
Saffron, Turmeric, Sumac, Vanilla, Juniper Berry
Some of the world's most expensive spices by weight — used in small amounts for big impact.
Saffron is the world's costliest spice by weight — it's the hand-plucked stigmas of a crocus, and it takes around 150 flowers to make a single gram.
Garam Masala, Ras el Hanout, Za'atar, Berbere, Chinese Five-Spice, Jerk Seasoning
Pre-balanced combinations that capture a regional cuisine's signature flavor in one jar.
'Ras el hanout' means 'head of the shop' — traditionally the very best spices a merchant had to offer, blended into a house signature that could run to 30 ingredients.
